title: _decimal: shared Context clear_flags() races repr()/.flags on the non-atomic mpd_context_t.status field
description: A shared decimal.Context embeds an mpd_context_t whose `status` (uint32_t) is read/written with plain accesses. ctx.clear_flags() writes CTX(self)->status = 0 (_decimal.c:1421) while a concurrent repr(ctx) reads ctx->status (_decimal.c:1570). Same non-atomic mpd_context_t::status field already filed as cpython#149142 (PR #150598) -> DUPLICATE, distinct call-site pair.
race_kind: unsynchronized shared struct field (non-atomic uint32_t; write-vs-read on mpd_context_t.status)
shared_state: PyDecContextObject.ctx.status (uint32_t condition flags, embedded mpd_context_t in a shared decimal.Context; also RMW'd via `ctx->status |= ...` at :616/:3498/:3513 by arithmetic, and read by the .flags property via signals_as_list :1722)
severity: low (crash-free: naturally-aligned uint32_t, no tearing, no pointer/refcount/lifetime; worst case is a stale/garbled flags in repr()/.flags or a lost flag bit from a racing |=)
repro_reliability: deterministic (exit 66; fires in a fraction of a second, 8 threads: 4 clear_flags writers vs 4 repr readers on one shared Context behind a Barrier)
suggested_fix: Same as #149142/#150598: make ctx->status/traps accesses atomic (FT_ATOMIC load/store + atomic fetch_or for the |= sites), or guard the Context field accessors (clear_flags, context_repr, flags/traps properties, arithmetic status-raise) with Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION(self).
notes: _decimal.c has ZERO Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION/_Py_atomic/FT_ATOMIC uses -> every Context field access is unsynchronized. Race is live/unfixed on current main (PR #150598 has not landed on this build). clear_flags-vs-repr is one concrete call-site pair of the broader mpd_context_t.status/traps non-atomic class.

related_issues.note: DUPLICATE OF THIS. '_decimal: mpd_context_t::status/traps mutated non-atomically leading to data race' (devdanzin, OPEN). PR #150598. Exactly the ctx->status field this race hits; clear_flags(write)/repr(read) is a second call-site pair of the same bug. Same fix resolves it.
PR for #149142 (makes mpd_context_t status/traps access atomic). Not yet landed on heads/main:bcf98ddbc40; race still reproduces.

assessment: DUPLICATE of #149142 (mpd_context_t.status non-atomic; PR #150598 pending). clear_flags-vs-repr is another call-site pair. Not for separate filing.
